weather_report:
Cottesloe wakes under a brooding sky, the sea turned to pewter and the air carrying that quiet, rain-scented hush. It feels mild and a touch humid, with the light flattened so that colors lean toward slate and blue. Sunshine will be scarce, more a pale glow behind cloud than a companion; the UV is mostly muted, though a passing tear in the overcast near midday could still nip at exposed skin.

Showers gather through late morning and settle into steady, moderate rain into the early afternoon, softening the horizon and smudging the view down the coast. It’s not an unkind day, just a contemplative one. A light waterproof over breathable layers will feel right, with comfortable closed shoes and a small umbrella to negotiate the slick footpaths. Think small pleasures: a window seat for a slow coffee, a quiet browse at the local shops between squalls, or a brief walk along the beach path when the rain slackens, watching the surf thicken and the gulls work the wind. Sunscreen can stay in the bag most of the time, but keep a dab handy in case the clouds briefly thin.
